name: New Year's Day
description: The first day of the 2026 calendar year.

name: Pongal / Makar Sankranti
description: Harvest observance marked across multiple Indian states.

name: Republic Day
description: National holiday commemorating the adoption of the Constitution of India.

name: Maha Shivaratri
description: Major Hindu observance dedicated to Lord Shiva.

name: Holi
description: Festival of colours celebrated across India.

name: Ugadi / Gudi Padwa
description: New year observance in several Indian states.

name: Ramzan Id / Eid al-Fitr
description: Festival marking the close of Ramadan; lunar date may shift with moon sighting.

name: Rama Navami
description: Hindu observance celebrating the birth of Lord Rama.

name: Mahavir Jayanti
description: Jain festival celebrating the birth of Mahavira.

name: Good Friday
description: Christian observance before Easter.

name: Vaisakhi
description: Harvest festival widely marked in Punjab and by Sikh communities.

name: Ambedkar Jayanti
description: Birth anniversary of Dr. B. R. Ambedkar.

name: Bohag Bihu
description: Assamese new year and spring festival.

name: Buddha Purnima
description: Buddhist observance of Gautama Buddha's birth and enlightenment.

name: Bakrid / Eid al-Adha
description: Festival of sacrifice; lunar date may shift with moon sighting.

name: Muharram / Ashura
description: Islamic observance at the beginning of the Hijri year; lunar date may shift.

name: Rath Yatra
description: Jagannath chariot festival observed in Odisha and elsewhere.

name: Independence Day
description: National holiday marking India's independence.

name: Onam
description: Major harvest festival in Kerala.

name: Milad un-Nabi
description: Islamic observance; lunar date may shift with moon sighting.

name: Raksha Bandhan
description: Festival celebrating sibling bonds.

name: Janmashtami
description: Hindu festival celebrating the birth of Lord Krishna.

name: Ganesh Chaturthi
description: Festival celebrating Lord Ganesha.

name: Mahatma Gandhi Jayanti
description: National holiday honoring Mahatma Gandhi.

name: Sharad Navratri
description: Nine-night Hindu festival observed across India.

name: Dussehra
description: Festival marking the triumph of good over evil.

name: Karwa Chauth
description: North Indian Hindu fasting observance.

name: Diwali / Deepavali
description: Festival of lights observed across India.

name: Govardhan Puja
description: Festival observed immediately after Diwali in many regions.

name: Bhai Duj
description: Festival celebrating sibling bonds after Diwali.

name: Chhath Puja
description: Major sun worship festival in Bihar, Jharkhand, and eastern India.

name: Guru Nanak Jayanti
description: Sikh festival celebrating the birth of Guru Nanak.

name: Christmas Eve
description: Evening observance ahead of Christmas Day.

name: Christmas
description: Christian festival commemorating the birth of Jesus Christ.
